title: Erie County Launches Renewable Diesel Pilot Program
content:
Erie County Executive Mark C. Poloncarz has launched a renewable diesel pilot program for the county's Public Works Department, an initiative first announced in his 2024 State of the County address. The program will test renewable diesel in three fleet vehicles and, if successful, will be expanded to the entire fleet.

The move is a strategic step towards reducing the county's carbon footprint, particularly in the heavy-duty sector where electrification is not yet feasible. Poloncarz noted that renewable diesel is a "drop-in ready" fuel that can reduce lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions by up to 90% and offers additional benefits, including lower tailpipe emissions for particulates and sulfur, which reduces health risks for workers.

To achieve greater efficiency and savings, Public Works Commissioner William Geary expressed interest in partnering with other municipalities and agencies to achieve economies of scale. He suggested that collective local commitment could get renewable diesel added to the New York State contract in 2026. Erie County, currently the only county in New York with an in-house program, mandated that the 5,000 gallons of fuel be free of palm oil. A full fleet conversion could reduce annual emissions by 6.6 million pounds.
author: Analysts Account
